message:
fix(defer): always emit structured DeferResult/UndeferResult object

br defer / br undefer used to switch the JSON/TOON/robot output shape
based on whether any issues were skipped:

  - 0 skipped: emit a bare list, e.g. [{"id":"bd-1",...}]
  - 1+ skipped: emit a result object,
    e.g. {"deferred":[...], "skipped":[...]}

The shape change broke downstream consumers and tests, since
parsers had to special-case "is this an array or an object?" for
otherwise identical successful runs. The variance also made the
two commands harder to script around than every other br command,
which always returns a stable envelope.

render_defer_output() and render_undefer_output() now unconditionally
build the DeferResult / UndeferResult struct (with an empty
skipped vec when none were skipped) and pass it to
emit_structured_output(). Because the struct uses
#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")] for `skipped` and
`ordered_outcomes`, the wire payload for the no-skip case stays
compact: {"deferred":[...]}.

Tests:

- New unit tests defer_result_serializes_as_object_without_skips and
  undefer_result_serializes_as_object_without_skips assert that the
  serialized form is an object with the array under "deferred" /
  "undeferred" and that "skipped" is omitted when empty.

- tests/e2e_env_overrides.rs: e2e_defer_honors_toon_env_mode now
  reaches into json["deferred"] instead of treating the whole
  payload as the array.

- tests/e2e_routing.rs: external-workspace defer/undefer routing
  asserts switch from deferred.as_array() to
  deferred["deferred"].as_array() (and the same for undefer).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
